enrichment.short_description: Backblaze offers unlimited personal cloud backup and low-cost, S3-compatible B2 object storage for developers and businesses.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (B2: 10GB free); Personal Backup from $9/month or $99/year; B2 Cloud Storage from $6/TB/month

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Backblaze is a San Mateo, California-based cloud storage company founded in 2007, publicly traded on the Nasdaq under the ticker BLZE. The company built its reputation on two distinct but complementary products: Backblaze Personal Backup (formerly Computer Backup), a flat-rate unlimited backup service for consumer and small-business computers, and B2 Cloud Storage, an S3-compatible object storage service aimed at developers, IT teams, and media/backup vendors who need affordable, scalable cloud storage without the complexity of hyperscaler pricing.

The two products share Backblaze's core philosophy: strip cloud storage down to a simple, predictable, low-cost offering. Personal Backup charges one flat fee per computer regardless of how much data is stored, while B2 charges a straightforward per-gigabyte rate with free or discounted egress through partner networks, positioning it as a much cheaper alternative to Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, or Azure Blob Storage for many workloads.

Backblaze is widely known in the tech press for its published hard drive reliability ('drive stats') reports, which have built long-standing trust in the brand's engineering credibility, and for powering the storage layer behind many third-party backup and media applications.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Backblaze Is For
paragraphs: Backblaze Personal Backup is a strong fit for individuals, freelancers, and small businesses who want a simple, set-and-forget backup for a Windows or Mac computer and its attached external drives, without worrying about storage limits or complicated folder selection rules. It's especially appealing to photographers, videographers, and anyone with large media libraries, since there's no cap on total data.

B2 Cloud Storage is well suited to developers, DevOps and IT teams, media and entertainment companies, and backup/archive software vendors (NAS makers, backup tools, media asset managers) that need S3-compatible object storage at a fraction of typical hyperscaler prices. It's also popular for storing backup targets from tools like Veeam, MSP360, and Synology, and for serving static assets or media via CDN integrations that offer free egress from B2.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Backblaze Personal Backup is not designed for full system-image/bare-metal restore, server backup, or backing up more than one computer per license — businesses needing centralized fleet management across many endpoints will find Backblaze Business or third-party MSP tools a better match, or may outgrow the consumer product's simplicity.

Organizations that need deep compliance certifications, advanced multi-region replication, or a fully-managed enterprise data platform with extensive native integrations (similar to AWS or Azure's broader ecosystems) may find B2's feature set comparatively lean, since Backblaze deliberately keeps the product focused on storage rather than a wide surrounding suite of compute and analytics services.

Teams already deeply invested in AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud's ecosystem, and who rely heavily on native services tightly coupled to those platforms, may not see enough benefit from migrating storage to a separate vendor despite the cost savings.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: Backblaze's Personal Backup automatically and continuously backs up an entire computer, including attached external drives, with no file-size or total-storage limits, plus 30-day version history (extendable) and the option to restore via download, a mailed USB drive, or the Backblaze mobile app. B2 Cloud Storage provides S3-compatible APIs, bucket-based object storage, lifecycle rules, and integrations with popular backup software, NAS devices, and CDNs.

Security features include private encryption keys (for Personal Backup) and server-side encryption for B2, along with two-factor authentication on Backblaze accounts. Business customers get centralized group management, deployment tools, and reporting through Backblaze Business Backup.

Because B2 uses a standard S3-compatible API, it plugs into a large existing ecosystem of tools without requiring custom integration work, which is one of the platform's most consistently praised technical strengths.

long_form_blocks.heading: The Backblaze Experience
paragraphs: Users regularly cite Backblaze's simplicity as its defining trait: installation is quick, backups run silently in the background, and pricing is easy to understand compared to usage-based competitors. The web and mobile interfaces for browsing and restoring files are considered clean and functional rather than flashy.

On the B2 side, developers appreciate the straightforward console, transparent pricing calculator, and detailed documentation, though some note that advanced enterprise tooling and support responsiveness can lag behind larger cloud providers. Overall, Backblaze is best known for delivering dependable, no-frills cloud storage at a price point that undercuts most alternatives.

features.title: Unlimited Personal Computer Backup
features.description: Continuous, automatic backup of an entire Windows or Mac computer with no cap on total data stored.

features.title: External Drive Backup
features.description: Automatically backs up files on attached external hard drives alongside the main computer.

features.title: B2 Cloud Storage (S3-Compatible)
features.description: Object storage with an S3-compatible API for developers, backup vendors, and media workflows.

features.title: Version History
features.description: Keeps prior versions of changed or deleted files for a configurable retention window.

features.title: Multiple Restore Options
features.description: Restore files via direct download, the Backblaze mobile app, or a mailed USB flash drive/hard drive.

features.title: Private Encryption Keys
features.description: Optional user-managed encryption key for Personal Backup, adding an extra layer of privacy control.

features.title: Business Backup & Group Management
features.description: Centralized deployment, group policies, and reporting for teams managing backups across multiple computers.

features.title: Free Egress Partnerships
features.description: B2 offers free or discounted data egress through partnerships with CDNs and compute providers like Cloudflare and Fastly.

features.title: Transparent, Usage-Based B2 Pricing
features.description: Simple per-gigabyte storage pricing with a free 10GB tier and a published cost calculator.

features.title: Third-Party Integrations
features.description: Works with backup software (e.g., Veeam, MSP360) and NAS devices (e.g., Synology) as a storage target.

pros: Truly unlimited storage for a flat monthly fee on Personal Backup, with no hidden data caps
B2 Cloud Storage is significantly cheaper per gigabyte than most major hyperscaler object storage
Simple, transparent pricing that's easy to estimate compared to complex usage-based cloud billing
S3-compatible API makes B2 easy to integrate with existing backup tools and workflows
Long-standing reputation for reliability, reinforced by Backblaze's public hard drive reliability reports
cons: Personal Backup covers only one computer per license, not a full multi-device or server backup solution
No bare-metal/full system-image restore for Personal Backup
B2's surrounding feature set is leaner than AWS/Azure/GCP's broader cloud ecosystems
Initial full backup and large restores can be slow depending on internet connection speed
Enterprise-grade support and advanced compliance tooling may lag behind larger cloud providers

faqs.question: Is Backblaze really unlimited storage?
faqs.answer: Yes, Backblaze Personal Backup provides unlimited backup storage for one computer, including its attached external drives, for a flat monthly or annual fee with no data cap.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Backblaze Personal Backup and B2 Cloud Storage?
faqs.answer: Personal Backup is a consumer-focused automatic backup app for a single computer, while B2 Cloud Storage is an S3-compatible object storage service billed per gigabyte, aimed at developers and businesses building their own storage integrations.

faqs.question: How much does Backblaze cost?
faqs.answer: Personal Backup starts at $9/month or $99/year per computer, while B2 Cloud Storage starts at $6/TB/month with the first 10GB free.

faqs.question: Does Backblaze support Mac and Windows?
faqs.answer: Yes, Backblaze Personal Backup has native apps for both Windows and Mac computers.

faqs.question: Is Backblaze B2 compatible with Amazon S3 tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, B2 Cloud Storage exposes an S3-compatible API, allowing many existing S3 tools, SDKs, and backup applications to connect to B2 with minimal changes.

use_cases.title: Personal computer disaster recovery
use_cases.description: Automatic, continuous backup of an entire computer and its external drives so files can be restored after hardware failure, theft, or ransomware.

use_cases.title: Low-cost off-site backup target for IT teams
use_cases.description: Using B2 as a cost-effective off-site storage destination for backup software like Veeam or Synology NAS backup jobs.

use_cases.title: Media asset storage and delivery
use_cases.description: Storing and serving large video, photo, or audio libraries from B2, often paired with a CDN for free egress.

use_cases.title: S3-compatible application storage
use_cases.description: Using B2's S3-compatible API as the object storage backend for custom applications, reducing cloud storage costs versus major hyperscalers.

features: Unlimited backup storage for one computer
Restore via download, app, or mailed drive
Deployment and reporting tools for teams

enrichment.short_description: Cloud HR platform for small and midsize businesses, combining HRIS, hiring, time off, and performance tools in one system.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: BambooHR is a cloud-based human resources platform built specifically for small and midsize businesses. Founded in 2008 and headquartered in Draper, Utah, the company was one of the earliest vendors to position a modern, self-service HRIS as an alternative to spreadsheets and legacy enterprise systems that were priced and designed for much larger organizations.

At its core, BambooHR centralizes employee records, hiring, onboarding, time-off tracking, and performance management in a single system of record. Over time it has expanded into adjacent areas, including a built-in applicant tracking system, a payroll add-on for US companies, benefits administration, and an AI assistant called Ask BambooHR that answers employee questions using company policy and HR data.

The platform has become one of the most recognized names in the SMB HR software category, frequently mentioned alongside Gusto, Rippling, and Paycor as a starting point for growing companies that need to move off manual processes without taking on the complexity of an enterprise HCM suite.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who BambooHR Is For
paragraphs: BambooHR is aimed at small and midsize companies, generally somewhere in the range of a few dozen to several hundred employees, that have outgrown spreadsheets, shared drives, or a patchwork of disconnected tools for storing employee data. It suits organizations that want a single system of record for HR rather than stitching together a separate applicant tracking tool, PTO tracker, and org chart.

It's a strong fit for HR generalists and small people-ops teams who need to cover hiring, onboarding, time off, and basic performance management without a large HR technology budget or a dedicated systems administrator. Companies that hire regularly benefit from having applicant tracking and onboarding built into the same platform as core employee records.

BambooHR also appeals to organizations that prioritize ease of use and employee self-service. Because managers and employees can request time off, look up the org chart, and check company information from a mobile app, it works well for distributed or partly remote teams that want HR information accessible outside a desktop HR portal.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Large enterprises with complex, multi-country payroll and compliance requirements, deep custom approval workflows, or advanced workforce planning needs will likely find BambooHR's feature depth and configurability more limited than dedicated enterprise HCM platforms built for that scale.

Companies that want fully transparent, self-serve pricing may find the quote-only sales process frustrating, since BambooHR does not publish per-employee rates and requires a sales conversation to get a quote. Very small teams with only a handful of employees may also find the per-employee cost structure less economical than lightweight, free, or flat-fee alternatives built for micro-businesses.

Organizations that need payroll outside the United States, sophisticated compensation planning, or advanced people analytics should expect to supplement BambooHR with additional specialized tools, since these areas are not its primary strength compared to broader HCM suites.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: At the core is BambooHR's HRIS: a centralized employee database with custom fields, an automatically generated org chart, e-signature document storage, and compliance tracking for things like I-9s and policy acknowledgments. This forms the system of record that other modules build on.

Hiring and workforce management are handled through a built-in applicant tracking system with customizable careers pages, interview scheduling, and offer letter templates, paired with structured onboarding checklists for new hires. Time-off tracking lets companies configure their own PTO policies and approval workflows, and an optional time tracking module covers hourly employee timesheets.

On top of the HR record, BambooHR offers performance management (goals, feedback, and review cycles), employee satisfaction and eNPS-style surveys, reporting and analytics with both prebuilt and custom reports, and a US-only payroll add-on. The newer Ask BambooHR AI assistant lets employees get quick answers to HR questions directly from the platform or mobile app, reducing routine tickets to the HR team.

long_form_blocks.heading: The BambooHR Experience
paragraphs: BambooHR is widely regarded as one of the more approachable HR platforms to learn, with a clean interface that doesn't require extensive training for HR admins, managers, or employees. Its iOS and Android apps let employees request time off, check the company directory, and view the org chart from their phones, which reduces the number of routine questions that land on the HR team.

Implementation typically involves guided setup and in-app help, and independent review sites consistently rate BambooHR's customer support among the stronger options in the SMB HR software space. The platform also connects to a marketplace of third-party integrations for payroll, benefits, and finance tools, plus an open API for teams that want to build custom connections.

The overall trade-off is one of breadth and simplicity versus deep configurability: BambooHR covers most of what a growing small or midsize business needs in one place and is easy to roll out, but companies with highly specialized or enterprise-scale HR requirements may eventually need more customizable or specialized systems.

features.title: Applicant Tracking System
features.description: Built-in hiring tools including customizable careers pages, interview scheduling, and offer letter templates.

features.title: Performance Management

features.title: Ask BambooHR (AI Assistant)
features.description: An AI-powered assistant that answers employee questions using company policies and HR data.

pros: Intuitive, easy-to-learn interface that speeds up onboarding for HR admins and employees alike
Combines core HRIS with a built-in applicant tracking system and onboarding tools in one platform
Mobile apps make self-service tasks like PTO requests and directory lookups convenient
Broad marketplace of third-party integrations plus an open API
Generally well-regarded customer support among SMB-focused HR platforms
cons: Pricing is quote-only with no published rates, making upfront cost comparison difficult
Payroll add-on is limited to companies operating in the United States
Total cost can rise noticeably as headcount grows and add-ons like Payroll or Time Tracking are layered on
Reporting and workflow customization are less deep than dedicated enterprise HCM suites
Some capabilities, such as performance management and payroll, require higher tiers or paid add-ons rather than being included by default

faqs.question: Does BambooHR include payroll?
faqs.answer: Payroll is available as a paid add-on, but it currently only supports companies running payroll in the United States.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for BambooHR?
faqs.answer: Yes, BambooHR offers a free trial (commonly around 7 days) so companies can test the platform before purchasing.

faqs.question: What size of company is BambooHR built for?
faqs.answer: It's designed primarily for small and midsize businesses, roughly from a few dozen up to several hundred employees, though larger organizations also use it.

faqs.question: Does BambooHR have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, BambooHR offers dedicated apps for iOS and Android that let employees and managers handle time off, the org chart, and directory lookups from their phones.

faqs.question: Can BambooHR integrate with other software?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers a marketplace of pre-built integrations with payroll, benefits, and finance tools, along with an open API for custom connections.

use_cases.title: Replacing spreadsheets with a single HR system of record

use_cases.title: Centralizing hiring and onboarding

use_cases.title: Formalizing performance reviews
